item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice scp. But a group like that couldn't possibly exist without rivals, right? And who wouldn't say these rivals could do the same thing the Children of the Torch could do. What if there is a whole array of elemental masks out there, waiting to be found by the Foundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The concept in itself is quite simple and kind of cliche, but the story is great… At least the parts that I understood.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Amazing article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I expect another update on the cult in series V, maybe one more if we make it to IX.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huǒjù zhi Zi

May I propose that it be changed to "火炬之子 huŏ jù zhī zĭ"? The former implies that "之" and "子" to be of neutral tone, which would read out differently due to Chinese being a tone-based language.

On that note, can we include the actual Chinese characters? Just using Hanyu Pinyin does not help much, since the same sounds can refer to multiple words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Excellent piece. However I have one gripe about the name of the organization. Huǒjù de Háizi(火炬的孩子）does literally translate into "Children of the Flame"。 However, it is a very basic and rudimentary translation, which takes out the immersion just a little a bit. If you want your group to sound "Ooo, mysterious. A secret society that existed for centuries!", you could use 火炬之子 huŏ jù zhī zĭ, which means exactly the same but just sounds a little more…ancient I guess.
Anyways, have my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It wasn't meant to be either. Some aspects of actual science get ignored at times because of X anomaly that makes it irrelevant. My problem with the original treatment here is that a scientist is regarding fire as something other than matter outside of any anomalous effects.

With the edit, I'll change my vote on the main article to neutral, but the supplement remains -1, poetic or not. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem could be fixed simply by changing the description to say "SCP-2814-1 is capable of interacting with fire as if it were a solid or a liquid." and striking the bit about "gaseous fire" from the supplement entirely.

I'm afraid that is not a simple solution, as it would involve removing the abstract and poetic elements mentioned later in the report about "gaseous flame."

In addition,

I know some people don't like to get actual science up in their sci-fi/fantasy SCPs,

I find this comment sarcastic and insulting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know some people don't like to get actual science up in their sci-fi/fantasy SCPs, but the fact that the description says that fire is not made of matter makes me downvote hard.
Specifically: "SCP-2814-1 is capable of interacting with fire as if it was comprised of matter."
"Fire" is a gas that is sufficiently excited during an oxidizing reaction that it emits radiation in the form of heat and light. Otherwise it would not interact with other matter in the way that it does (blowing around in wind, being extinguished by solids or liquids, etc). The chemical reaction is present, but the reaction produces the glowing gas (or alters existing gas). You might as well say the coals in a campfire aren't composed of matter either, since they are undergoing the same reaction.

In the supplemental document, there is this: "It doesn't seem, however, that the mask can create "gaseous" fire. When I tried, the ball in my hands shuddered and exploded into a fine mist; perhaps that's the closest it can get to actual gas."
If he tried to make gaseous fire, he would get…regular fire.

The problem could be fixed simply by changing the description to say "SCP-2814-1 is capable of interacting with fire as if it were a solid or a liquid." and striking the bit about "gaseous fire" from the supplement entirely.

As it stands, downvote on both for something that breaks my suspension of disbelief more than a magic mask that makes you pyrokinetic and crazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doctor Hoffman's comment is fine, Agent Edvin. Chill.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do try and control your fandom

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts exactly, actually. Good group with a good continuity, though I might try and get some more story line with potentially other authors established before considering it a GoI. And like TayJK said, I'd suggest changing the link a little. I'd also tack on that a little more physical description couldn't hurt. Otherwise, good enough for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted entirely because the number of casualties is 108.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm liking these Children of the Torch SCPs, sounds like a good GoI without a worldwide reach, which I have a complaint of for more potential GoI: Many of them are at Foundation power level. This seems like a group that, if they became a bit more of a problem, the Foundation could wipe out IF they made it a stand up fight. But this is more of a Guerilla GoI. Anyways, I digress.

I am not a fan of links being the word here. Sounds less official document, more '90s Geocities. Instead of 'click here,' try consult (link) Document-2814-Gamma.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Give it to me now. I want NEED to be a fire bender.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In all fairness, those tests are only routine by the standards of artifacts belonging to this cult, not necessarily Foundation-wide.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This gets my upvote, not necessarily because I find the object itself that interesting, because it's not that far removed from "ye olde creepy mask what does things", which is pretty well-tread ground. What I did like was the level of detail in constructing the underlying story, and the general notion of some crazy group that fucks with the goddamn sun. I like the sort of ominous nature of what exactly "resurrecting our Radiant Father" entails, so I guess in a way my upvote is given in expectation that you'll bring this home in a satisfying manner.

I do have to say, I found the "routine human immolation tests" mention in the addendum document a little pointlessly grimdark, even for my tastes. Even at the Foundation, I don't imagine people are generally that flippant about burning people to death.

Otherwise, this was executed well, and I found myself entertained. Good work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, the only bit of this I liked was the "we cannot resurrect our Radiant Father this way" in the addendum. The rest wasn't terribly interesting. The descriptions in the related document were slightly more interesting, but overall that doesn't do anything for me either. (It's a little too "why the hell did we let a researcher wear a skip?")

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ SPOILERS
- SPOILERS

This is a direct sequel to SCP-1428.

The cult mentioned here constructed 1428-2.

The Radiant Father is our sun and is sometimes depicted as a three-armed humanoid, like at 1428-2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cross referencing like this would be insanely frowned upon, but I would love to see what would happen between a -1 and SCP-457. (The burning man)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 108 is a special number in East Asian religion/mysticism/ect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: He does?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…

Is..is this some sort of Chinese offshoot of the Fifthists? Burning, stars…this looks about right. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, it's a fire bending mask? And it indoctrinates you to the fire nation? I can dig it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Presently it is believed that the fatalities produced by SCP-2814, while in the group's possession, total at 108."

Iiiiiiiiiiii see what you did there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh I wouldn't do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I bet the whole thing is really Keter and the Foundation just hasn't figured it out yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Btw, this is actually the second article in a series I started over a year ago (the name of the first article is the very last word in the supplement).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This article is hot!

I dunno, man, insert your own, better pun here if you've got one.

I like this one. It was fairly apparent that the guy was going to go crazy once he put on the mask, but I think you executed it pretty well. Also, nicely executed Chekhov's Gun with the "gaseous flame" thing.
